pages.title: Contact - Derek Twigg MP
pages.meta_description: Member of Parliament for Widnes and Halewood                                     (Including Whiston, Hale Village, Cronton, Halebank & Tarbock)
pages.text_excerpt: Contact - Derek Twigg MP Skip to main content Derek Twigg MP Member of Parliament for Widnes and Halewood (Including Whiston, Hale Village, Cronton, Halebank & Tarbock) Get In Touch Join Donate Home About Derek Contact Our Constituency Derek in Parliament EDM Policy Hough Green & Ditton Neighbourhood Plan More Search Search the website Search Menu Contact There is a strict Parliamentary protocol that states that Members of Parliament can only raise issues for their own constituents. If you are unsure whether I am your MP, you can check who your MP is by clicking here. If you are a constituent, please use the below contact details, and a member of my team will get back in touch with you: Email: derek.twigg.mp@parliament.uk (please include your name, address and phone number) Phone: 0151 424 7030 (Calls are endeavoured to be answered Monday – Friday 9.30am – 4pm. However, the phone line can be busy and I have a small team. If you receive the answer machine message, please leave a message, including your name, address and phone number). There is also a 24 hour answer machine. Please note that if you are contacting on behalf of another individual, in most cases, their direct consent will be required. I respect your right to privacy. The personal information that I hold about you is governed by legal regulations. My Privacy Policy sets out what information I hold, for what reason, how it is stored and how you can query this with me. You can read my Privacy Policy here . IMPORTANT: By completing this form, you give me permission to contact you and use the information that you have provided to make representations on your behalf to other organisations. Derek Twigg MP Contact details Email: derek.twigg.mp@parliament.uk Further details Promoted by Andrea Wall on behalf of Derek Twigg both C/O Suite E, Cheshire House, Gorsey Lane, Widnes, WA8 0RP ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: About Derek - Derek Twigg MP
pages.meta_description: Member of Parliament for Widnes and Halewood                                     (Including Whiston, Hale Village, Cronton, Halebank & Tarbock)
pages.text_excerpt: About Derek - Derek Twigg MP Skip to main content Derek Twigg MP Member of Parliament for Widnes and Halewood (Including Whiston, Hale Village, Cronton, Halebank & Tarbock) Get In Touch Join Donate Home About Derek Contact Our Constituency Derek in Parliament EDM Policy Hough Green & Ditton Neighbourhood Plan More Search Search the website Search Menu About Derek Photo of Derek Twigg MP speaking in the House of Commons Derek was born and bred in the Constituency and studied at Halton College of Further Education ( Riverside College). Dismayed by the Labour Government’s defeat in the 1979 General Election, Derek joined the Labour Party. By the age of 21 he was elected to represent the Broadheath and Kingsway wards on Cheshire County Council, winning the seat back from the Tories. That election saw the Conservatives lose their majority on the Council for the first time. In a by-election in 1984 Derek won the Halton View ward seat on Halton Borough Council where he focused on his main concerns: housing and poverty. By the age of 27 he had become Chairman of the Housing Committee where he vigorously pursued a policy of modernising Halton’s housing stock and increased tenant participation. He later served as Chairman of Finance and led on Education during Halton’s successful bid to become a Unitary Authority. In 1997 Derek succeeded the Rt Hon Gordon Oakes as Member of Parliament for Halton winning with a record majority of over 23,000. On 10th June 1997 Derek made his maiden speech in the House of Commons in the Education (Schools) Bill debate ( read Derek’s maiden speech. ) Derek has served as a member of the Public Accounts Committee. He has been Parliamentary Private Secretary to two ministers, Firstly, to Rt Hon Helen Liddell who was then Minister of State for Energy, and then to Rt Hon Stephen Byers when he was Secretary of State for Transport and the Regions. In June 2002 Derek became a Government Whip. In December 2004 Derek was appointed as Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Schools at the Department for Education and Skills. After the May 2005 General Election, Derek was assigned the role of Parliamentary Under Secretary of State at the Department for Transport. In September 2006 Derek was appointed as Under Secretary of State for Defence and Minister for Veterans at the Ministry of Defence. Derek left the Government in October 2008, and became a member of the Children, Schools & Families Select Committee. Derek served as a Shadow Health Minister in October 2010, returning to the back benches in summer 2011. From 2013 until 2015 he sat as a member of the Defence Select Committee. Derek later returned to the Defence Select Committee and is currently a member of it. Since the General election in July 2024, he has been appointed by the Prime Minister as Leader of the UK Delegation to the NATO Parliamentary Assembly and a member of the Intelligence and Security Committee. He is also a member of the Joint Committee on National Security Strategy. Derek’s main interests outside of politics include hill walking, military history, football and Rugby League. Derek Twigg MP Contact details Email: derek.twigg.mp@parliament.uk Further details Promoted by Andrea Wall on behalf of Derek Twigg both C/O Suite E, Cheshire House, Gorsey Lane, Widnes, WA8 0RP ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: EDM Policy - Derek Twigg MP
pages.meta_description: Member of Parliament for Widnes and Halewood                                     (Including Whiston, Hale Village, Cronton, Halebank & Tarbock)
headings: h2: Why I don’t sign EDMs
pages.text_excerpt: EDM Policy - Derek Twigg MP Skip to main content Derek Twigg MP Member of Parliament for Widnes and Halewood (Including Whiston, Hale Village, Cronton, Halebank & Tarbock) Get In Touch Join Donate Home About Derek Contact Our Constituency Derek in Parliament EDM Policy Hough Green & Ditton Neighbourhood Plan More Search Search the website Search Menu EDM Policy Why I don’t sign EDMs I regularly get requests from constituents for me to sign what are called Early Day Motions (EDMs) of the House of Commons. I write back explaining why I don’t sign EDMs. There are a number of reasons why I don’t sign EDMs. The main one, is that they have no effect and there are numerous other ways which are effective in which I can raise an issue or lobby the Government. Signing EDMs requires virtually no effort. Signing EDMs has become a displacement activity for doing something useful like writing personally to the Minister, raising the issue in questions or debates in Parliament, applying for a debate, tabling amendments to Bills etc – all of which require much more time and effort. The tax payer also picks up a substantial bill for the cost of processing the hundreds of EDMs tabled each month. This is why I took the decision many years ago to never sign EDMs on principle. However, that does not mean the issues raised are not important and instead I write personally to Ministers raising the issues and asking them to respond, as well as raising issues in other useful and effective ways. Derek Twigg MP Contact details Email: derek.twigg.mp@parliament.uk Further details Promoted by Andrea Wall on behalf of Derek Twigg both C/O Suite E, Cheshire House, Gorsey Lane, Widnes, WA8 0RP ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved.
